phrasalVerbs.definition: To start doing something in a very enthusiastic way, often without much preparation.
examples: She dived in and started reorganising the entire department.
Don't be afraid to dive in and try something new.
He tends to dive in without considering the consequences.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To make less progress or be less successful than other people who are doing a similar job or activity.
examples: If you miss too many classes, you'll fall behind.
The company fell behind its competitors in innovation.
Students who fall behind often struggle to catch up.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To start working hard, especially when you should have done this earlier.
examples: It's time to knuckle down and finish the project.
She finally knuckled down after months of procrastination.
The team needs to knuckle down if we're going to meet the deadline.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To end someone's employment, especially temporarily, because there is not enough work for them.
examples: The factory laid off hundreds of workers during the recession.
She was laid off when the company downsized.
Many employees fear being laid off due to automation.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To decide not to take part in something, or to stop taking part in it.
examples: Several countries opted out of the agreement.
You can opt out of receiving marketing emails at any time.
He decided to opt out of the company pension scheme.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To spend a particular amount of time doing something or make a particular amount of effort.
examples: She puts in extra hours every week to meet her targets.
You'll need to put in a lot of effort to pass this exam.
The team put in an outstanding performance.

phrasalVerbs.definition: To study something very hard, especially for an examination.
examples: I need to swot up on my grammar before the test.
She spent the weekend swotting up on company history.
He's been swotting up on the new regulations.

fillInExercises.sentence: She didn't hesitate and just ___ right away.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Dive in' means to start something enthusiastically and immediately.

fillInExercises.sentence: If you don't study regularly, you'll ___ behind the other students.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Fall behind' means to make less progress than others.

fillInExercises.sentence: It's time to ___ and focus on your dissertation.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Knuckle down' means to start working hard.

fillInExercises.sentence: The company had to ___ off several employees due to budget cuts.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Lay off' means to end someone's employment, often due to lack of work.

fillInExercises.sentence: You can ___ of the newsletter subscription at any time.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Opt out' means to choose not to participate in something.

fillInExercises.sentence: He ___ in over 60 hours of work last week.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Put in' means to spend time or effort on something.

fillInExercises.sentence: I need to ___ on the new software before the training session.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Swot up on' means to study something intensively.

fillInExercises.sentence: After the holidays, it's hard to ___ down and get back to work.
fillInExercises.explanation: 'Knuckle down' means to start working hard and seriously.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I heard the company might [lay off] some staff next month.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: That's concerning. We should [knuckle down] and prove our value.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: I've been [put in] extra hours to stay ahead of deadlines.

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Good idea. We can't afford to [fall behind] our colleagues.

lines.speaker: Sarah
lines.text: Should we [swot up (on)] the new systems they're implementing?

lines.speaker: Carlos
lines.text: Definitely. Let's [dive in] and become experts before everyone else.

lines.speaker: Priya
lines.text: I'm thinking of [opt out (of)] the company's share scheme.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Really? You should [swot up (on)] the benefits before deciding.

lines.speaker: Priya
lines.text: I know, but I've [fall behind] on my savings recently.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Maybe you need to [knuckle down] and create a budget.

lines.speaker: Priya
lines.text: You're right. I'll [put in] more effort to manage my finances.

lines.speaker: Dan
lines.text: Don't just [dive in] though—plan it carefully first.
